column2: What are your general observations/impressions about the Batangas State University institutional visit?
column3: What AI tools/technology or facilities from the Batangas State University stood out as relevant to your office's work or the Academy's offerings?
column4: What potential activities, practices, or approaches from the visit can be adopted or adapted for our programs and offerings?
column5: What possible areas of collaboration with the Batangas State University do you see (e.g. training, research, joint projects, access to facilities)?
column9: Rate your overall experience of the Institutional Visit. (1-Poor, 5-Excellent)
column10: Any additional recommendations or ideas for the next steps?

column2: My overall impression of the institutional visit to Batangas State University is positive. The discussions showed the University's strong focus on research, innovation and collaboration.  VP Amante's team demonstrated clear direction of their programs, and the facilities presented during the visit reflected their readiness to support future partnerships. The visit offered useful insights into the institution's priorities and potential areas for cooperation.
column3: The AI tools and facilities that stood out were those focused on data analytics, machine learning, and research support. Their labs are useful for our work in digital initiatives and can complement the Academy's that introduce emerging technologies to public sector leaders.
column4: Their approach to showcasing applied research and technology-driven solutions can also be adapted to enrich our program delivery and enhance participants' practical learning experiences.
column5: Access to their specialized laboratories and facilities can support capacity-building initiatives and technical learning components of our programs.
column10: A recommended next step is to formalize the potential areas of collaboration through a follow-up meeting to identify specific activities and timelines. Establishing clear coordination points from both sides will help ensure smooth planning and implementation.

column2: My overall impression of the Batangas State University visit is very positive. I did not expect their advanced AI, data, and future technology initiatives. The visit showed that the university is forward-thinking, innovative, and well-prepared for emerging technologies.
column3: The AI tools and technology that stood out the most to me were those showcased at the STEER Building. The facilities demonstrated how advanced AI, data, and technology infrastructures can significantly enhance program delivery.  In relation to our office’s work and the Academy’s offerings, I believe DAP would greatly benefit from having a dedicated floor focused on AI, data, and emerging technologies. I also recommend that we invest further in upgrading and strengthening our IGH and related facilities to align with current technological advancements.
column4: We can adopt some of their internal practices, particularly in strengthening our data infrastructure, server setup, and internet system monitoring. These improvements can support more efficient operations and better program delivery.
column5: Possible areas of collaboration include joint projects or partnerships in research, training, and technology or AI-related initiatives, where both institutions can share expertise and resources.
column10: Thank you for the invitation. I look forward to another institutional visit and similar learning opportunities in the future.

column2: They possessed a clear and compelling vision for their future and took deliberate steps to realize it. This included substantial investments in emerging and future-ready technologies.
column3: The tools they use, along with the processes and metrics they follow in managing their ICT operations—from server management to software application development.
column4: Perhaps we can gather the geotags of our clients’ offices so we can visually map the geographic reach of our interventions.
column5: Possible areas of collaboration could include offering Technical Assistance on ICTD good practices, particularly in strengthening systems, processes, and digital governance capacities.
column10: Perhaps for the next institutional visit, a pamphlet can be provided containing a brief description of the facility, its core functions, key projects, and contact information.
